title: eriogonum trachygonum var. subscaposum
intro: Eriogonum trachygonum var. subscaposum, commonly known as a desert buckwheat or a type of sagebrush, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Eriogonum trachygonum var. subscaposum include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for wound healing and pain relief. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, improve digestion, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Eriogonum trachygonum var. subscaposum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
